title: CLITORIA TERNATEA
description: Clitoria ternatea, commonly known as Butterfly Pea or Shankhpushpi, is a perennial leguminous herb rich in anthocyanins, flavonol glycosides, and triterpenoids. It functions primarily as a nootropic and adaptogen by modulating neurotransmitters and enhancing hippocampal long-term potentiation to improve cognitive function and stress resilience.

research.title: Clitoria ternatea L. root extract ameliorated the cognitive and hippocampal long-term potentiation deficits induced by chronic cerebral hypoperfusion in the rat
research.summary: This study demonstrates that root extracts can restore memory function and strengthen synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus, particularly in models of cognitive decline.

research.title: Clitorienolactones and Isoflavonoids of Clitorea ternatea Roots Alleviate Stress-Like Symptoms in a Reserpine-Induced Zebrafish Model
research.summary: Research identified specific lactones and isoflavonoids that effectively reduce physiological stress behaviors and restore neurochemical balance.

research.title: Age dependent neuroprotective effects of medhya rasayana prepared from Clitoria ternatea Linn. in stress induced rat brain
research.summary: The plant shows significant neuroprotective properties against stress-induced brain aging by reducing oxidative markers and supporting neuronal health.

research.title: The Ayurvedic medicine Clitoria ternatea--from traditional use to scientific assessment
research.summary: A comprehensive review confirming the plant's wide-ranging pharmacological activities, including nootropic, anxiolytic, and anticonvulsant effects.

sentiment.summary: Users generally value Clitoria ternatea as a reliable component of traditional cognitive stacks, noting its calming yet brain-boosting effects without the heavy sedation of other herbs.

effects.label: Nootropic Potency:
effects.description: Highly effective when used as part of a 'Medhya Rasayana' (brain tonic) formula

effects.label: Stress Reduction:
effects.description: Proven effective in animal models for reversing stress-like symptoms including lethargy

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Works well when combined with other adaptogens like Ashwagandha and Bacopa

dosage.label: Traditional Extract:
dosage.description: Common dosages in mixed formulas range around 112.5 mg of root powder or extract

dosage.label: Root vs Flower:
dosage.description: Roots are traditionally more concentrated for cognitive benefits, while flowers are used for antioxidant teas

dosage.label: Standard Preparation:
dosage.description: Often consumed as 'Shankhapushpi' syrup or capsules in Ayurvedic medicine

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Upset:
sideEffects.description: Some users may experience mild indigestion if taken on an empty stomach

sideEffects.label: Sedative Potential:
sideEffects.description: High doses may act as a mild tranquilizer or sedative in sensitive individuals

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Caution is advised when combining with prescription anticonvulsants or sedatives

availability.label: Traditional Availability:
availability.description: Widely available in Asian markets and health stores as 'Butterfly Pea' tea or powder

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Frequently found in complex Ayurvedic 'Manomitram' or focus-oriented stacks
